text.title: retention-metrics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for retention-metrics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Computing DAU from event_count > 0 instead of COUNT(DISTINCT user_id).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-session users inflate DAU; the metric becomes non-comparable across time periods.

failure-mode.description: Reporting D30 retention for cohorts that are only 10 days old.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Diagonal effect: newest cohorts have incomplete history; D30 comparisons are meaningless until the cohort matures to 30+ days.

failure-mode.description: Comparing cohort retention across periods with different traffic mixes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A paid-channel expansion confounds the trend; always segment by acquisition source before declaring retention improvement.

failure-mode.description: Churn-risk model trained on Q1 data scoring Q3 cohorts without retraining.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Product changes and seasonal patterns shift the feature distributions; a stale model misclassifies users and wastes intervention budget.

failure-mode.description: Push-notification spikes from re-engagement campaign counted as "retention improvement".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Notification-induced short-term returns must be separated from habitual return — distinguish them by checking D7 retention of reactivated users vs organic baseline.
